stories.headline: UK Banks Set to Extend Rally Against EU Peers, Berenberg Says
stories.summary: UK bank stocks are well placed to catch up to the loftier valuations of European peers in coming years as they benefit from above-par growth in profitability and stronger capital generation, according to Berenberg analysts.

stories.headline: Stock Rout Pauses, Europe Heat Wave Intensifies | The Pulse 6/24/2026
stories.summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Joyce Chang, JPMorgan Global Research Chair; Clemens Fuest, Ifo Institute President; Miljan Gutovic, Holcim CEO; Benoit Bazin, Saint-Gobain CEO.    (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: US Exceptionalism Nearing Expiration Date, HSBC’s Kettner Says
stories.summary: Max Kettner, chief multi-asset strategist at HSBC, says investors may want to be “perhaps really leaning a bit more into that European story,” as he sees an August expiration date on US exceptionalism. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Investors Maxing Out AI Trade Feed Stock Market Volatility

stories.headline: South Korea Delays Single-Stock Weekly Options Days Before Start
stories.summary: South Korea has delayed a plan to launch weekly single-stock options on shares including SK Hynix Inc. and Samsung Electronics Co., the latest sign regulators are becoming jittery after wild stock-market swings.

stories.headline: Indian Bonds Rally as RBI Governor Says Rate Hike Talk Premature
stories.summary: India’s benchmark bond yields and swap rates fell to their lowest levels in three months as the nation’s central bank Governor pushed back against expectations for tighter monetary policy.

stories.headline: Indian State Lenders Said to Seek $2 Billion in Overseas Loans
stories.summary: Some Indian state-run financial institutions are planning to raise overseas loans of as much as $2 billion using a central bank facility for borrowing at concessional rates, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Korea’s Surging Equity Volatility Draws Meme-Stock Comparisons
stories.summary: The volatility in South Korea’s benchmark equity index has become so extreme that investors and analysts are likening the market’s surging intra-day swings to the meme-stock frenzy.

stories.headline: Copper Holds Losses as Dollar and Hawkish Fed Pressure Metals
stories.summary: Copper and industrial metals largely held Tuesday’s slump as a stronger dollar and the Federal Reserve’s hawkish stance weighed on the demand outlook.

stories.headline: Trump to Meet With G.O.P. Senators Amid New Divisions

stories.headline: Moderate Democrat Wins Primary in Utah for Newly Blue Salt Lake City Seat
stories.summary: Ben McAdams, a former representative who had to distance himself from more conservative positions he once held, will be favored to win a blue seat created by court-ordered redistricting.

stories.headline: Local Republican Wins Primary for Nancy Mace’s House Seat in South Carolina
stories.summary: Jenny Costa Honeycutt, a Charleston County councilwoman and lawyer, topped a Republican rival in a runoff primary election for a seat that leans to the right but could be competitive in November.
